content.json:
# mdk - operator workbook (on-call)

**Persona:** on-call operator ("is the runtime healthy right now?").

This workbook is the **first** thing to open when an SLO alert fires. It maps 1:1 to the **golden-signals Grafana dashboard** (`dashboards/grafana/mdk-golden-signals.json`) but uses **KQL against the workspace-based App Insights `App*` tables** that the OTel Collector's `azuremonitor` exporter writes to (ADR 020). Scope this workbook to the Log Analytics workspace the deployment writes to (`logs.outputs.workspaceId` in `infra/azure/main.bicep`).

**Alert rules surfaced here** (from `infra/azure/modules/monitor-alerts.bicep`):

- `*-deadletter-spike` (any dead-letter in 15m)
- `*-high-error-rate` (>10% over 15m)
- `*-high-latency-p95` (p95 > 30s over 15m)
- `*-availability-no-traffic` (zero successful API requests in 15m)

**Cross-surface.** Same signals, PromQL side: `dashboards/grafana/mdk-golden-signals.json`. Choose Workbook when you want Azure Portal SSO + alert-rule pivot; choose Grafana for multi-cloud + live Jaeger trace links.

links.linkLabel: Open Azure Monitor Alerts blade (item-27 SLO rules)

content.json:
## 1. Health overview

**What.** Top-line operator KPIs over the dashboard time-range: `agent.execute` failure rate (`AppDependencies`) + p95 latency (`AppDependencies.DurationMs`).

**Normal.** Error % < 1; p95 < 2000 ms.

**If red, do.**
- error % rising over 10 -> **`*-high-error-rate`** is firing; jump to the recent-failures panel below and pivot on `ResultCode` / `OperationName`.
- p95 rising over 30000 ms -> **`*-high-latency-p95`** is firing; jump to the latency heatmap below + the `eval-and-drift` workbook if a model regression is suspected.

_ADR refs: ADR 020 (Azure exporter), ADR 015 (Langfuse drilldown by operation_Id)._

content.query:
AppDependencies
| where Name == "agent.execute" and AppRoleName == "movate-runtime"
| summarize Total = count(), Failed = countif(Success == false), P95 = percentile(DurationMs, 95)
| extend ErrorPct = iff(Total == 0, 0.0, round(100.0 * Failed / Total, 2))
| project Total, Failed, ErrorPct, P95Ms = P95
content.title: agent.execute - total / failed / error% / p95 (window)

content.json:
## 2. Active alerts

**What.** Currently-firing alerts scoped to this resource group / subscription. Surfaces the four item-27 SLO rules + any drift-side rules.

**Normal.** Empty table.

**If red, do.** Click through to the alert; the rule description carries the exact condition (e.g. `agent.execute failure rate over PT15M exceeded 10%`).

content.query:
AlertsManagementResources
| where type =~ 'microsoft.alertsmanagement/alerts'
| extend severity = tostring(properties.essentials.severity), monitorCondition = tostring(properties.essentials.monitorCondition), alertState = tostring(properties.essentials.alertState), targetResource = tostring(properties.essentials.targetResource), startDateTime = todatetime(properties.essentials.startDateTime), name = tostring(properties.essentials.alertRule)
| where monitorCondition == 'Fired' and alertState != 'Closed'
| project name, severity, alertState, startDateTime, targetResource
| order by startDateTime desc
content.title: Firing alerts (Resource Graph)

content.json:
## 3. Recent failures

**What.** Failed `agent.execute` invocations in the window (`AppDependencies.Success == false`). Includes the `OperationId` you can paste into the Application Insights transaction search to see the full trace.

**Normal.** Empty or trickle of non-retried provider 5xx.

**If red, do.**
- group concentrated on one `ResultCode` -> provider-side; check provider status page.
- spread across codes after a deploy -> revert or pin; correlate with the Container Apps revision history in the platform workbook.
- KB-heavy agents predominate -> open the `eval-and-drift` workbook to see whether eval pass-rate regressed.

content.query:
AppDependencies
| where AppRoleName == "movate-runtime" and Success == false
| where Name in ("agent.execute", "workflow.execute")
| project TimeGenerated, Name, DurationMs, ResultCode, OperationId, AppRoleInstance, Properties
| order by TimeGenerated desc
| take 100
content.title: Recent failed agent.execute / workflow.execute (last 100)

content.json:
## 4. Latency heatmap

**What.** `agent.execute` `DurationMs` distribution over time (heatmap). Hot bands at the top are the tail; banded hot rows are the SLO-breaching regions.

**Normal.** Heat concentrated under ~2000 ms with a thin tail to ~5000 ms.

**If red, do.**
- Hot band > 30000 ms -> the **`*-high-latency-p95`** SLO is firing; cross-link Grafana's `p95 by kind` to see which `kind` is the regression.
- Slow drift of the median upward over hours -> KB chunk-size regression or provider degradation; run `mdk eval`.

content.query:
AppDependencies
| where Name == "agent.execute" and AppRoleName == "movate-runtime"
| make-series DurationCount = count() default = 0 on TimeGenerated step 5m by bin(DurationMs, 500)
| order by DurationMs asc
content.title: agent.execute DurationMs heatmap (5m bins, 500ms buckets)

content.json:
## 5. Throughput by status

**What.** `mdk.jobs.completed` summed by `status` over 5-minute bins. Direct equivalent of the Grafana stacked-throughput panel.

**Normal.** Steady `success` line; `error` < 1% of total; `dead_letter` flat at zero.

**If red, do.** Any non-zero `dead_letter` -> **`*-deadletter-spike`** is or will fire. Pull the failing run from the recent-failures table above and replay from Langfuse.

content.query:
AppMetrics
| where Name == "mdk.jobs.completed"
| extend status = tostring(Properties["status"])
| summarize Jobs = sum(Sum) by status, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| order by TimeGenerated asc
content.title: mdk.jobs.completed by status (5m bins)
